title: A Busy Week: Dates and Questions
learningGoal: The learner will read a 350-word diary entry to parse English calendar vocabulary, differentiate between numerical spelling (teens vs tens), and identify inverted syntax used in questions.
instructions: Read the following diary entry. Notice how the writer uses capital letters for days and months. Pay special attention to the numbers: 'fourteen' (14) vs 'forty' (40). Lastly, notice how the subject and verb flip when asking a question (e.g., 'Where am I?'). Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: Try to read aloud to practice your new words!

readingTitle: A Busy Week: Dates and Questions
readingText: Today is Monday, October twelve. It is a very busy day. Who is in the house? I am alone. My father has forty emails to read. He is forty-five years old. My sister is fourteen years old. She is at school. Where is my mother? She is at the market. She has forty rupees. What is in my bag? Let me look. I have five books. I do not have fifty books! That is too heavy. Tomorrow is Tuesday. Tuesday is my favorite day. Where are we going on Tuesday? We are going to the park. My friend Amit is coming. Amit is thirteen years old. He has three dogs. They are very fast. When is Wednesday? Wednesday is the day after Tuesday. It is a slow day. I am taking an English test on Wednesday. Is it a difficult test? No, it is not difficult. What is my favorite month? My favorite month is December. December is very cold, but it is happy. August is very hot. Now it is October. Who are you? You are my new friend. You are reading my diary! Are you happy? I hope you are happy. Let us practice English numbers together. Ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety, entirely up to one hundred! Count the days. Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Every week has seven days. Every year has twelve months. When are you coming to my house? Please come soon. Have a great day!

That is too heavy. Tomorrow is Tuesday. Tuesday is my favorite day. Where are we going on Tuesday? We are going to the park. My friend Amit is coming. Amit is thirteen years old. He has three dogs. They are very fast. When is Wednesday? Wednesday is the day after Tuesday. It is a slow day. I am taking an English test on Wednesday. Is it a difficult test? No, it is not difficult. What is my favorite month? My favorite month is December. December is very cold, but it is happy. August is very hot. Now it is October. Who are you? You are my new friend. You are reading my diary! Are you happy? I hope you are happy. Let us practice English numbers together. Ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety, entirely up to one hundred! Count the days. Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Every week has seven days. Every year has twelve months. When are you coming to my house? Please come soon. Have a great day!
baseLanguageSupportText: आज सोमवार, बारह अक्टूबर है। यह बहुत व्यस्त दिन है। घर में कौन है? मैं अकेला हूँ। मेरे पिता को पढ़ने के लिए चालीस ईमेल हैं। वह पैंतालीस वर्ष के हैं। मेरी बहन चौदह वर्ष की है। वह स्कूल में है। मेरी माँ कहाँ है? वह बाज़ार में है। उसके पास चालीस रुपये हैं। मेरे थैले में क्या है? मुझे देखने दें। मेरे पास पांच किताबें हैं। मेरे पास पचास किताबें नहीं हैं! वह बहुत भारी है। कल मंगलवार है। मंगलवार मेरा पसंदीदा दिन है। हम मंगलवार को कहाँ जा रहे हैं? हम पार्क जा रहे हैं। मेरा दोस्त अमित आ रहा है। अमित तेरह वर्ष का है। उसके पास तीन कुत्ते हैं। वे बहुत तेज़ हैं। बुधवार कब है? बुधवार मंगलवार के बाद का दिन है। यह एक धीमा दिन है। मैं बुधवार को एक अंग्रेजी की परीक्षा दे रहा हूँ। क्या यह एक कठिन परीक्षा है? नहीं, यह कठिन नहीं है। मेरा पसंदीदा महीना कौन सा है? मेरा पसंदीदा महीना दिसंबर है। दिसंबर बहुत ठंडा होता है, लेकिन यह खुशनुमा होता है। अगस्त बहुत गर्म होता है। अभी अक्टूबर है। तुम कौन हो? तुम मेरे नए दोस्त हो। तुम मेरी डायरी पढ़ रहे हो! क्या तुम खुश हो? मुझे आशा है कि तुम खुश हो। चलो एक साथ अंग्रेजी संख्याओं का अभ्यास करते हैं। दस, बीस, तीस, चालीस, पचास, साठ, सत्तर, अस्सी, नब्बे, पूरे एक सौ तक! दिन गिनें। सोमवार, मंगलवार, बुधवार, गुरुवार, शुक्रवार, शनिवार, रविवार। हर सप्ताह में सात दिन होते हैं। हर साल में बारह महीने होते हैं। तुम मेरे घर कब आ रहे हो? कृपया जल्दी आना। तुम्हारा दिन शुभ हो!

exampleSentence.baseTranslation: एक साल में बारह महीने होते हैं।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: My sister is fourteen years old.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: माई सिस्टर इज़ फोर्टीन इयर्स ओल्ड।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: मेरी बहन चौदह वर्ष की है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Where are we going on Tuesday?
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: वेयर आर वी गोइंग ऑन ट्यूसडे?
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: हम मंगलवार को कहाँ जा रहे हैं?
क्या यह एक कठिन परीक्षा है? (Inverted verb 'Is it' vs normal 'It is')

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: When are you coming to my house?
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: वेन आर यू कमिंग टू माई हाउस?
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: तुम मेरे घर कब आ रहे हो?

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Based on the text, how old is the sister?
How many books are in the writer's bag?
What WH-word does the writer use to ask about the mother's location ('___ is my mother?')
True or False: According to the text, 'Are you happy?' is a correct question format.
What does the writer use at the start of a question to ask about time? ('____ are you coming to my house?')

expectedSummaryPoints: The text forces the reader to distinguish 'Fourteen' from 'Forty' contextually.
Mondays, Tuesdays, October, and August are all capitalized properly.
The writer demonstrates question inversion: 'Is it' instead of 'It is'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You clearly understood the subtle spelling difference between 'teen' and 'ty' numbers and perfectly mapped the WH-question words to their contexts.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep at it! You might have confused 'fourteen' (14) with 'forty' (40). Go back and read the numbers carefully.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember that 'Where' asks about location (माँ कहाँ है?) and 'When' asks about time (तुम कब आ रहे हो?).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Today is Monday, October twelve... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Read question sequences slowly. Notice that it says 'Is it a difficult test?' rather than 'It is a difficult test?' The verb is flipped!
commonMistakes: Thinking the sister is 40 years old.
